name: UpCloud Cloud Infrastructure API
what_it_does: European cloud infrastructure provider with REST API — enabling developers and businesses to provision high-performance cloud servers (MaxIOPS NVMe storage), managed Kubernetes clusters, object storage, load balancers, managed databases, and private networking via a developer-friendly API, with data centers across Europe and a strong focus on performance and simplicity.
use_cases: Provisioning UpCloud cloud servers via API for automated infrastructure deployment
Managing UpCloud Managed Kubernetes clusters via API for k8s infrastructure automation
Configuring UpCloud object storage (S3-compatible) via API for data storage automation
Managing UpCloud load balancers and private networks via API for networking setup
Querying UpCloud server metrics and billing via API for infrastructure monitoring
not_for: Serverless and managed service breadth (UpCloud is IaaS — not AWS-equivalent)
Teams requiring US-East-coast data centers (UpCloud is European-centric)
Developer PaaS deployment (use Railway, Render, or Fly.io for PaaS)
best_when: A developer or startup needs a high-performance, developer-friendly European cloud with MaxIOPS NVMe storage, managed Kubernetes, and S3-compatible object storage — at competitive pricing with excellent documentation.
avoid_when: You need AWS-breadth managed services, US-centric data centers, or serverless compute.

auth.notes: HTTP Basic auth with username + password (account credentials or API sub-user). Sub-users can be created with limited permissions. No OAuth2. No API key separate from credentials — uses account credentials or sub-user credentials. No webhooks — poll for resource state. S3-compatible storage uses AWS-style access key + secret. SDKs for Go, Python, JavaScript, Ansible.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier; $25 credit for new accounts
pricing.paid_tiers: Cloud servers from €5/month; Managed Kubernetes from €0.015/hour; Object storage from €0.01/GB/month; Databases from €25/month
pricing.notes: Finnish company (Helsinki). Founded 2011. Private company. Headquartered in Helsinki with offices in London. 12 data centers globally (EU-focus: FIN, GER, NL, UK, PL, ES; + US, SG, AUS). Known for MaxIOPS NVMe storage architecture (consistently fastest in benchmarks). ISO27001 certified. GDPR compliant. 99.999% uptime SLA (higher than most competitors). Managed databases (PostgreSQL, MySQL, Redis). Managed Kubernetes with automatic upgrades. S3-compatible object storage. Strong developer experience focus.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API v3 with good developer docs. HTTP Basic auth (account or sub-user). SDKs for Go, Python, JS, Ansible. No webhooks — must poll. GDPR and ISO27001 compliant. 99.999% SLA (industry-leading). No separate API key — credentials-based auth. Credit card required.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Server reads idempotent by UUID. Storage reads idempotent. Network reads idempotent. Server creation not idempotent — check by hostname. State change operations idempotent for same desired state.
known_agent_gotchas: HTTP Basic auth with account credentials — create sub-users for limited-permission agents
No API key separate from credentials — use sub-user credentials for agents
No webhooks — must poll for server and task state changes
Credit card required even for pay-as-you-go ($25 credit on signup)
S3-compatible object storage uses DIFFERENT auth (AWS-style) from server API

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR, ISO27001 compliant. HTTP Basic auth (username + password) — use sub-users with minimal permissions. Sub-user permission scoping available (some granularity). 99.999% SLA. Private networking option. S3-compatible storage with separate AWS-style credentials. Finnish company — EU jurisdiction. ISO27001 certification. No OAuth2 or fine-grained API scopes.
